findings.title: New LibraryTabBar component has no unit or integration tests
findings.why_it_matters: LibraryTabBar is the primary IA control for the entire Library page overhaul. It has 4 conditional branches (active vs inactive tab styling per tab), click handlers with callbacks, aria-selected state, focus-visible accessibility outlines, and a role='tablist' ARIA contract. None of these behaviors are verified by any test. The component is 46 lines of pure presentational logic with map-rendered buttons -- it is trivially unit-testable with React Testing Library or even plain DOM assertions, but no test file exists for it.
evidence: src/app/components/library/LibraryTabBar.tsx (NEW file, 46 lines, zero test coverage)
No LibraryTabBar.test.tsx, LibraryTabBar.spec.ts, or LibraryTabBar.test.ts exists anywhere in the repo
No E2E test clicks a tab button to verify switching -- all tests navigate with ?tab=browse URL param instead
findings.suggested_fix: Add a libreact component test for LibraryTabBar that verifies: (1) all 4 tabs render with correct labels, (2) active tab has aria-selected='true' with bg-card class, (3) inactive tabs have aria-selected='false' with text-muted-foreground class, (4) clicking a tab fires onChange with the correct tab id, (5) tablist has aria-label='Library sections', (6) focus-visible outline renders on keyboard focus

findings.title: Tab state persistence logic (URL param priority, localStorage fallback, default) has zero tests
findings.why_it_matters: The tab initialization in Library.tsx (lines 138-150) implements a 3-tier fallback: URL param `?tab=` overrides localStorage key `knowlune-library-tab`, which overrides the hardcoded default `'continue'`. This is a state machine with multiple branches. No test exercises any of the non-browse paths. `handleTabChange` writes to localStorage on every tab switch, and this write side-effect is also untested. A regression that breaks URL param parsing, localStorage reading, or the default fallback would go undetected. The localStorage key `knowlune-library-tab` appears nowhere in the test suite.
evidence: src/app/pages/Library.tsx lines 138-145: useState initializer with urlTab, localStorage.getItem, and default 'continue'
src/app/pages/Library.tsx lines 146-149: useCallback handleTabChange writing to localStorage.setItem
Zero test files reference 'knowlune-library-tab' localStorage key
All 16 test files exclusively use ?tab=browse; no test navigates to ?tab=continue, ?tab=collections, or ?tab=history
No test seeds localStorage key 'knowlune-library-tab' to test the fallback path
findings.suggested_fix: Add E2E tests for: (1) ?tab=collections sets Collections as active tab on load, (2) localStorage fallback works when no URL param present (seed 'knowlune-library-tab': 'collections', navigate to /library, assert collections tab panel is visible), (3) invalid URL param falls through to localStorage then default, (4) clicking a tab writes to localStorage with correct key and value

findings.title: test.fixme in story-116-03.spec.ts eliminates all shelf integration verification
findings.why_it_matters: The only E2E spec written to validate E116-S03 acceptance criteria (shelf headings as h2, shelf-row scrollers, cards per row, 'See all' links, no console errors) was changed from `test(...)` to `test.fixme(...)` meaning it is now skipped during test runs. E116-S03 is marked 'done' in sprint-status.yaml despite having zero running tests that prove shelves render correctly on the Library page. The shelves were relocated inside the `libraryTab === 'continue'` conditional panel, and the test that proved they render at all is now silently skipped.
evidence: tests/e2e/story-116-03.spec.ts line 17: test.fixme('renders two shelves with headings, scrollers, and cards', ...)
docs/implementation-artifacts/sprint-status.yaml: '116-3-library-page-shelf-integration: done'
No other test file verifies shelf headings, shelf scrollers, shelf cards, or 'See all' links render on the Library page
findings.suggested_fix: Either (1) fix the shelf rendering issue that caused the test to be marked fixme and re-enable it, updating it to navigate to /library?tab=continue, or (2) write a new spec that proves AC-2, AC-3, AC-4, AC-8, and AC-9 for the shelf integration. mark E116-S03 as 'done/pending-test-repair' until coverage is restored.

findings.title: Continue, Collections, and History tabs have zero direct E2E test coverage
findings.why_it_matters: The Library page refactor moved content from unconditional rendering into 4 conditional tab panels. Only the Browse tab has any coverage via existing tests (all navigating with ?tab=browse). The Continue, Collections, and History tabs have NO tests at all. Their data-testid wrappers (library-tab-continue, library-tab-collections, library-tab-history) appear zero times in the entire test suite. This means the following features are only proven to work when they were monolithic -- there is no proof they still work inside their new tab context: the media hero and shelves (Continue), shelf management and ABS collections (Collections), yearly stats and recently finished list (History).
evidence: src/app/pages/Library.tsx: data-testid='library-tab-continue' (line 744), data-testid='library-tab-collections' (line 1057), data-testid='library-tab-history' (line 1124)
grep for these data-testids in tests/ returns 0 results
All 16 test files use ?tab=browse; zero test files exercise ?tab=continue, ?tab=collections, or ?tab=history
History tab (lines 1123-1221): stats cards + recently finished list + empty state -- completely untested
Collections tab (lines 1056-1118): Manage Shelves button, Browse ABS Collections button, SmartGroupedView, empty state -- completely untested
Continue tab content (LibraryFormatModeTabs, LibraryMediaHero, ReadingQueue, DailyHighlightsStrip) is only tested indirectly via old monolithic tests that predate the tab refactor
findings.suggested_fix: Add smoke tests for each tab: (1) /library?tab=continue: assert library-tab-continue is visible, format tabs render, hero or empty state renders, (2) /library?tab=collections: assert manage-shelves-inline button is visible, SmartGroupedView or empty state renders, (3) /library?tab=history: assert 3 stats cards render, recently finished section renders or shows empty state

findings.title: New ebooks-only fallback in media-first format default logic is untested
findings.why_it_matters: Library.tsx lines 380-391 changed the format default from always setting ['audiobook'] to a two-path fallback: prefer audiobooks if present, else fall back to ebooks. The ebooks-only path (user has only epub/pdf books, no audiobooks) is not tested. A bug in the ebooks filter assignment (setting wrong format array, missing the format filter entirely, or crashing on empty books array) would go undetected.
evidence: src/app/pages/Library.tsx lines 380-391: useEffect with hasAudiobooks check and ebook fallback
The useEffect dependency array changed from [books.length, filters.format, setFilter] to [books.length, filters.format, setFilter, books]
No test seeds only ebook books (no audiobooks) and verifies the format filter defaults to ['epub', 'pdf']
findings.suggested_fix: Add test that seeds only ebook-format books (no audiobooks), navigates to /library, and asserts that the format filter is set to ['epub', 'pdf'] or that ebook book cards are visible (while audiobook-only content is not)

findings.title: No test verifies content scoping between tabs -- browse content could leak to continue tab
findings.why_it_matters: With conditional rendering per tab (libraryTab === 'continue', libraryTab === 'browse', etc.), the rendering guards are purely logical but untested in any cross-tab scoping scenario. If a condition regression causes browse content (filters, grid, series view) to render on the Continue tab, no test would catch it because all tests navigate to ?tab=browse where browse content IS expected to render. Conversely, if Continue tab content (hero, shelves) renders on Browse tab, no test verifies it shouldn't be there.
evidence: src/app/pages/Library.tsx: all 4 tab panels are conditionally rendered with `books.length > 0 && libraryTab === '<tab>'`
No test: navigates to ?tab=continue, then asserts that browse-specific data-testids (e.g., local-view-toggle, series-view) are NOT present
No test: navigates to ?tab=browse, then asserts that continue-specific data-testids (e.g., library-tab-continue child content) is NOT present
findings.suggested_fix: Add a scoping test: navigate to /library?tab=continue, assert library-tab-continue is visible and library-tab-browse is not. Then navigate to /library?tab=collections, assert library-tab-collections is visible and the other tab wrappers are absent.

findings.title: No accessibility test for tab bar keyboard navigation or ARIA attributes
findings.why_it_matters: LibraryTabBar uses role='tablist', role='tab', aria-selected, and aria-label, but no test verifies these attributes render correctly or that keyboard navigation (Tab to focus, Arrow keys to switch, Enter/Space to activate) works. ARIA compliance is especially important for a primary navigation control like the tab bar.
evidence: src/app/components/library/LibraryTabBar.tsx: role='tablist', role='tab', aria-selected, aria-label
No test for keyboard navigation between tabs
No test for aria-selected attribute correctness
No test for focus-visible outline style on keyboard interaction
findings.suggested_fix: Add E2E test: press Tab to focus active tab, press ArrowRight to move focus to next tab, press Enter to activate, verify the correct tab panel appears. Or add a unit test that renders LibraryTabBar and verifies ARIA attributes on the rendered DOM.

residual_risks.risk: Tab switching via UI click could be entirely broken -- all tests bypass the tab bar by navigating with URL params
residual_risks.mitigation: Add at least one E2E test that clicks a tab button (library-tab-collections) and verifies the correct panel renders

residual_risks.risk: localStorage corruption or schema mismatch for knowlune-library-tab could silently break tab persistence for all users
residual_risks.mitigation: Add a test that writes to localStorage, reloads the page, and verifies the correct tab is active

residual_risks.risk: E116-S03 shelf rendering may be broken after tab refactor -- the only test was disabled via test.fixme
residual_risks.mitigation: Re-enable or rewrite the shelf integration spec with correct ?tab=continue navigation and verify shelves render

residual_risks.risk: History tab content (yearly stats computation) is entirely unverified -- wrong year filter, missing null-safety on finishedAt, or incorrect sorting could produce wrong results
residual_risks.mitigation: Add a test that seeds books with specific finishedAt dates and verifies the stats cards show correct counts

residual_risks.risk: Deletion confirmation dialog text changed from mentioning 'highlights' to generic 'permanently remove' -- tests were updated to match, but no test verifies that highlights ARE actually still deleted alongside the book
residual_risks.mitigation: This is a behavioral regression risk separate from testing quality: verify the deletion implementation still cleans up highlights

testing_gaps.gap: No unit or integration tests for LibraryTabBar component
No tests for tab state initialization: URL param priority, localStorage fallback, default 'continue'
No E2E tests for Continue tab panel content (LibraryMediaHero, LibraryMediaShelfColumn, ReadingQueue, DailyHighlightsStrip)
No E2E tests for Collections tab panel content (Manage Shelves inline, Browse ABS Collections, SmartGroupedView, empty state)
No E2E tests for History tab panel content (yearly stats cards, recently finished list, empty state)
No test for format default when only ebooks exist (no audiobooks in library)
No test for content scoping -- verifying browse content doesn't render on continue tab and vice versa
Shelf integration test (E116-S03 AC coverage) disabled via test.fixme -- all 5 acceptance criteria untested
